days.title: Day 1
days.summary: Set up the Python learning environment and establish the basic habits students will use for the rest of the course: running code, printing output, naming values, and evaluating expressions.

hours.title: Environment setup
hours.summary: Install or open the classroom Python environment, confirm the interpreter works, and practice the edit-run-debug loop.

hours.title: Printing and literals
hours.summary: Use print(), string literals, numbers, comments, and predictable output formatting.

hours.title: Variables and basic types
hours.summary: Store values with clear names and distinguish strings, integers, floats, and booleans.

hours.title: Operators and expressions
hours.summary: Combine values with arithmetic, assignment, and comparison-friendly expression patterns.

outcomes: Run Python code in the classroom environment.
Use print() to produce deterministic output.
Create and update variables with clear names.
Evaluate simple expressions with common operators.

days.title: Day 2
days.summary: Build confidence with text processing, user input, type conversion, and the first checkpoint for core Python syntax.

hours.title: String fundamentals
hours.summary: Index, slice, and reason about immutable text values.

hours.title: String methods
hours.summary: Clean, transform, search, split, and join strings using common methods.

hours.title: Input and conversion
hours.summary: Read user input and convert string input into numbers safely for calculations.

hours.title: Checkpoint 1
hours.summary: Apply variables, strings, operators, input, and conversion in integrated exercises.

outcomes: Manipulate strings with indexing, slicing, and methods.
Convert text input to numeric types.
Complete a checkpoint that combines the first two days.

days.title: Day 3
days.summary: Use comparisons and booleans to make decisions about data, format output with f-strings, and develop early debugging habits.

hours.title: Comparisons and Boolean logic
hours.summary: Compare values and combine conditions with and, or, and not.

hours.title: F-string formatting
hours.summary: Create readable output by embedding expressions and formatting values.

hours.title: Split and join text
hours.summary: Convert between strings and lists of text fragments for simple parsing tasks.

hours.title: Debugging habits
hours.summary: Read errors, trace values, and fix syntax or logic mistakes systematically.

outcomes: Write Boolean expressions from plain-language rules.
Use repeatable debugging steps for beginner errors.

days.title: Day 4
days.summary: Introduce lists as the first major collection type and practice traversing, updating, and organizing sequence data.

hours.title: List fundamentals
hours.summary: Create lists, access elements, update values, and use common list methods.

hours.title: Iterating with for loops
hours.summary: Loop over collections to inspect and transform repeated values.

hours.title: Nested lists
hours.summary: Represent table-like data with lists of lists and index nested structures.

hours.title: Checkpoint 2
hours.summary: Combine list traversal with string handling in practical exercises.

outcomes: Use for loops to process collections.
Complete a list and string handling checkpoint.

days.title: Day 5
days.summary: Compare Python's core collection types and learn when tuples, sets, and dictionaries are the clearest fit.

hours.title: Tuples and unpacking
hours.summary: Use immutable grouped values and unpacking for fixed-shape data.

hours.title: Sets
hours.summary: Track uniqueness, membership, and set operations.

hours.title: Dictionary fundamentals
hours.summary: Map keys to values and retrieve data by meaningful identifiers.

hours.title: Dictionary iteration
hours.summary: Loop through keys, values, and items while practicing counting patterns.

outcomes: Choose between list, tuple, set, and dictionary structures.
Use sets for uniqueness and membership checks.
Apply dictionary iteration and counting patterns.

days.title: Day 6
days.summary: Strengthen collection fluency through structure-selection practice, mini-project work, and a checkpoint that connects data structures to small programs.

hours.title: Choosing structures
hours.summary: Select the right collection type for common beginner problem shapes.

hours.title: Data-structure drill circuit
hours.summary: Practice rapid collection transformations and lookups.

hours.title: In-memory tracker mini-project
hours.summary: Build a small tracker that stores and updates records while the program runs.

hours.title: Checkpoint 3
hours.summary: Demonstrate collection selection and manipulation across integrated tasks.

outcomes: Build a small in-memory data tracker.

days.title: Day 7
days.summary: Control program flow with conditional logic, compound Boolean expressions, nested decisions, and modern match/case syntax.

hours.title: if, elif, and else
hours.summary: Branch program behavior based on conditions.

hours.title: Nested conditions
hours.summary: Organize multi-step decisions without losing readability.

hours.title: Boolean operators
hours.summary: Combine conditions and simplify decision rules.

hours.title: match/case
hours.summary: Use pattern-style branching where it makes beginner code clearer.

days.title: Day 8
days.summary: Use while loops and loop-control tools to validate input and build a small command-line contact manager.

hours.title: while loops
hours.summary: Repeat work until a condition changes.

hours.title: Loop control
hours.summary: Use break, continue, and pass intentionally in beginner programs.

hours.title: Input validation
hours.summary: Keep prompting until user input satisfies program rules.

hours.title: CLI mini-project
hours.summary: Assemble loop and collection skills into a menu-driven contact manager.

outcomes: Validate user input with repeated prompts.

days.title: Day 9
days.summary: Introduce functions as reusable program units and organize related helpers into simple modules.

hours.title: Function basics
hours.summary: Define functions with parameters and return useful values.

hours.title: Scope and common mistakes
hours.summary: Understand local variables, return flow, and beginner function pitfalls.

hours.title: Functions with collections
hours.summary: Pass lists and dictionaries into helpers and return processed results.

hours.title: Modules and utils.py
hours.summary: Import standard modules and create a small local utility module.

outcomes: Use parameters and return values correctly.
Create and import a local helper module.

days.title: Day 10
days.summary: Move from procedural scripts into object-oriented thinking with classes, objects, attributes, methods, and basic inheritance.

hours.title: Class definitions
hours.summary: Define simple classes and instantiate objects.

hours.title: Attributes and methods
hours.summary: Store object state and attach behavior to that state.

hours.title: Inheritance basics
hours.summary: Reuse and specialize behavior through parent and child classes.

hours.title: Checkpoint 4
hours.summary: Demonstrate class design and object usage in small exercises.

days.title: Day 11
days.summary: Persist data with files and make programs more robust with explicit exception handling.

hours.title: Reading files
hours.summary: Open text files and read data safely with context managers.

hours.title: Writing files
hours.summary: Write deterministic text output and understand overwrite versus append behavior.

hours.title: try and except
hours.summary: Catch expected errors and keep programs understandable.

hours.title: Error management
hours.summary: Combine file handling and exceptions in practical workflows.

outcomes: Read text files with context managers.
Combine file I/O and error handling.

days.title: Day 12
days.summary: Finish the Basics module with capstone delivery, final review, certification preparation, and course wrap-up.

hours.title: Capstone project
hours.summary: Plan and assemble a small program that uses the module's core skills.

hours.title: Capstone polish
hours.summary: Improve readability, validation, and output consistency.

hours.title: PCEP-style review
hours.summary: Review core syntax and beginner certification topics.

hours.title: Final wrap-up
hours.summary: Demonstrate outcomes and identify next steps into Advanced Python.

outcomes: Connect beginner skills into full scripts.

days.title: Day 1
days.summary: Kick off the Advanced module by turning requirements into class designs, protecting object state, and choosing composition or inheritance intentionally.

hours.title: Advanced kickoff
hours.summary: Reconnect the Basics foundation to the applied project track and module expectations.

hours.title: Class design from requirements
hours.summary: Translate nouns, verbs, and rules into object models.

hours.title: Invariants and exceptions
hours.summary: Protect object state and signal invalid operations clearly.

hours.title: Composition vs inheritance
hours.summary: Choose reuse patterns that keep project code maintainable.

days.title: Day 2
days.summary: Use design patterns and type hints to make class-heavy code easier to extend, test, and read.

hours.title: Factory pattern
hours.summary: Centralize object creation when construction choices vary.

hours.title: Strategy pattern
hours.summary: Swap behavior without rewriting the caller.

hours.title: Class ergonomics and typing
hours.summary: Use type hints and small APIs to make objects easier to use.

hours.title: Checkpoint 1
hours.summary: Apply patterns to a domain plus service layer milestone.

outcomes: Recognize Factory and Strategy use cases.
Improve class APIs with type hints.

days.title: Day 3
days.summary: Organize growing projects with packages, logging, safer file operations, and decorators for cross-cutting behavior.

hours.title: Project structure
hours.summary: Organize packages and modules for an applied Python project.

hours.title: Logging
hours.summary: Replace print debugging with structured runtime information.

hours.title: Context managers
hours.summary: Use with statements for safer file and resource handling.

hours.title: Decorators
hours.summary: Wrap functions for timing, validation, authorization, or instrumentation.

days.title: Day 4
days.summary: Connect Python programs to HTTP workflows and plan the capstone's persistence-ready core.

hours.title: HTTP client work
hours.summary: Make requests, inspect responses, and handle common client-side concerns.

hours.title: Security basics
hours.summary: Discuss secrets, input validation, and safe boundaries in API-adjacent code.

hours.title: Capstone planning
hours.summary: Define the persistence-ready core and data flow for the applied project.

hours.title: Checkpoint 2
hours.summary: Save and load JSON while preserving the core domain model.

days.title: Day 5
days.summary: Introduce desktop GUI development with Tkinter and ttk, including layout, validation feedback, and list-oriented interfaces.

hours.title: Tkinter fundamentals
hours.summary: Create windows, widgets, and event-driven callbacks.

hours.title: Layout systems
hours.summary: Arrange controls with predictable spacing and resizing behavior.

hours.title: Validation feedback
hours.summary: Surface form errors and success states in the interface.

hours.title: Record list interfaces
hours.summary: Show project records and connect selection to actions.

days.title: Day 6
days.summary: Wire GUI workflows into project logic, separate controllers, clean up callbacks, and polish the mini-project checkpoint.

hours.title: GUI CRUD wiring
hours.summary: Connect create, read, update, and delete actions to interface controls.

hours.title: Controller separation
hours.summary: Keep UI code from absorbing business rules.

hours.title: Callback cleanup
hours.summary: Refactor event handlers into readable, testable units.

hours.title: Checkpoint 3
hours.summary: Polish the GUI mini-project and confirm the interaction flow.

outcomes: Connect GUI actions to project logic.

days.title: Day 7
days.summary: Move from in-memory data to sqlite persistence with schema thinking, CRUD operations, row mapping, and transactions.

hours.title: Schema thinking
hours.summary: Design tables and columns around project data requirements.

hours.title: sqlite CRUD
hours.summary: Create, read, update, and delete records with sqlite.

hours.title: Row mapping
hours.summary: Convert database rows into project-friendly Python objects or dictionaries.

hours.title: Transactions and initialization
hours.summary: Create database setup routines and protect multi-step writes.

outcomes: Initialize and transact against a database.

days.title: Day 8
days.summary: Extend database work with ORM or deeper SQL practice, integration, search, pagination, and the persistence checkpoint.

hours.title: ORM or deeper SQL
hours.summary: Compare abstraction choices for database-backed Python projects.

hours.title: Database integration
hours.summary: Connect persistence functions to the project service layer.

hours.title: Search and pagination
hours.summary: Retrieve usable subsets of records for UI and API workflows.

hours.title: Checkpoint 4
hours.summary: Demonstrate database-backed project behavior.

outcomes: Choose an appropriate database integration style.

days.title: Day 9
days.summary: Expose project behavior through RESTful endpoints with Flask, serialization, validation, and service wiring.

hours.title: REST fundamentals
hours.summary: Map resources, methods, status codes, and payloads.

hours.title: Flask setup
hours.summary: Create a small Flask app and route requests.

hours.title: CRUD endpoints
hours.summary: Expose create, read, update, and delete operations over HTTP.

hours.title: Serialization and validation
hours.summary: Convert project data to JSON and reject invalid payloads.

days.title: Day 10
days.summary: Harden the API milestone with security basics, Python client functions, integration choices, and a checkpoint.

hours.title: API security basics
hours.summary: Discuss authentication boundaries, secrets, and safe defaults.

hours.title: Python client functions
hours.summary: Call API endpoints from reusable Python helpers.

hours.title: Integration choices
hours.summary: Decide how GUI, database, and API pieces should communicate.

hours.title: Checkpoint 5
hours.summary: Validate the API milestone against project requirements.

days.title: Day 11
days.summary: Use data analysis libraries to inspect, visualize, and interpret project-related data.

hours.title: Pandas essentials
hours.summary: Load, inspect, filter, and summarize tabular data.

hours.title: Matplotlib visualizations
hours.summary: Create basic plots that communicate data patterns.

hours.title: Regression demo
hours.summary: Explore a practical modeling example without losing focus on interpretation.

hours.title: Analytics integration
hours.summary: Connect data summaries back to the capstone project story.

days.title: Day 12
days.summary: Finish Advanced with pytest, coverage, packaging, runnable delivery, final capstone demos, and certification-style review.

hours.title: pytest fundamentals
hours.summary: Write tests for functions, classes, and project behavior.

hours.title: Coverage and packaging
hours.summary: Check test coverage and prepare a runnable project structure.

hours.title: Final capstone demo
hours.summary: Present the integrated project and explain technical decisions.

hours.title: Certification-style review
hours.summary: Review advanced topics and identify next learning steps.

outcomes: Measure coverage and package runnable code.
